mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS > 137k and UNRATE < 10% fails to produce positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts (HOUST) following a multi-month decline signals improved consumer confidence and demand for durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where HOUST shows a 2%+ 3-month rebound.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust corporate capacity utilization, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where INDPRO shows a 1%+ monthly increase.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, which increase discount rates and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) during high unemployment (UNRATE > 10%) signals weak demand conditions, which correlate with negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE > 10%, RSAFS declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in payroll employment (PAYEMS) during rising unemployment (UNRATE > 11%) indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, which drive negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE > 11%, PAYEMS declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4% during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) signals aggressive monetary tightening, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%, 20-day forward returns are positive in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).
